{{Quote|So the next time you find yourself having to visit some musty archives on Janilis VII, be smart—send your assistant instead. Just make sure he's equipped with a RTDS.|Advertisement for the RTDS-1000 remote text data scanner in the fiftieth-anniversary edition of ''Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology''|<ref name="Galladinium">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]''</ref>}}
'''Janilis VII''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] situated in the [[Cronese Mandate/Legends|Cronese Mandate]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]. It was one of the cradles of the [[Cronese]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] civilization and was part of the [[Kingdom of Cron]]. Janilis VII hosted several archives that were still in existence twenty-five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] later, although the planet itself had become rundown and its society was engaged in subsistence economy.

==Description==
Janilis VII, also known simply as Janilis, was a lush [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the seventh [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="Xim" /> in the [[Janilis system/Legends|Janilis system]].<ref name="TEA" /> It was a part of the [[Cronese Mandate/Legends|Cronese Mandate]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]],<ref name="Xim" /> within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA" /> Janilis VII was situated between the [[Arcan system/Legends|Arcan]] and [[Chandaar system/Legends|Chandaar systems]] on the [[Greater Cronese Arc]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="Xim" />

==History==
[[File:KingdomOfCron25150BBY-TheHistoryOfXimAndTheTionCluster.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Janilis VII was part of the Kingdom of Cron.]]
The Janilis [[Star system/Legends|system]] was [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] by [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Sleeper ship/Legends|sleeper ships]] at some point after approximately [[27,500 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> Janilis VII was one of the cradles of the Human [[Cronese]] civilization, being favored by Cronese [[Noble/Legends|nobles]] and constituting a part of the core territory of the [[Kingdom of Cron]] by around [[25,150 BBY]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the reign of [[King of Cron]] [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] that began around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Janilis VII was already an old world and hosted several archives. Those still remained in operation around the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] twenty-five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] later, with the Cronese charging steep admission fees from researchers searching for lost documents from Xim's [[Pre-Republic era|era]].<ref name="Xim" />

At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'' is set during the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the events of {{Film|IV}} and {{Film|V}}, which corresponds to some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Janilis VII was mentioned in the [[Galladinium's 50th anniversary edition|fiftieth-anniversary edition]] of ''[[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology]]''. In the [[Datalog/Legends|datalog]], an advertisement for [[Willienk Data Systems]]' [[RTDS-1000]] [[remote text data scanner]] suggested that, if the reader were faced with the need to investigate "musty" archives on the planet, they should instead send an assistant equipped with an RTDS-1000.<ref name="Galladinium" />

==Inhabitants==
Originally settled more than twenty-five thousand years prior to the reign of the Galactic Empire, by the latter time Janilis VII still remained a populous world, although it had become rundown, with its inhabitants practicing subsistence economy.<ref name="Xim" />

==Behind the scenes==
Janilis VII was first mentioned in ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galladinium" /> The [[August 18]], [[2009]]<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/185066/the-essential-atlas-star-wars-by-daniel-wallace-and-jason-fry|text=The Essential Atlas: Star Wars}}</ref> reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Janilis system, and therefore Janilis VII, in grid square S-6,<ref name="TEA" /> after which the [[December 7]], 2009 [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]<ref name="XimOriginal">{{SWArchive|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek01/index.html|text=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> article "[[Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster]]" by [[Jason Fry]] introduced the shortened name "Janilis."<ref name="Xim" />
